rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component or implementation scope, preserving the AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in component type annexes and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use visible identifiers when possible; avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Ensure AGREE type names are valid (int, real, bool) and align with the expression type
generic_rules: Preserve the original AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use visible identifiers in contracts to maintain traceability and avoid implementation-specific paths
Align AGREE type declarations with the actual expression type to prevent type mismatches
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when using visible identifiers
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic in AGREE contracts
anti_patterns: Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not use opaque Boolean data ports in range comparisons or 0/1 equality checks
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers in AGREE contracts
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
